item.title: INFRA: Markdown Parser (CommonMark + GFM)
item.intent: The parser is the foundation — every rendering, formatting, doctor, and export feature depends on a correct AST. Get this wrong and everything downstream is broken.
item.description: Integrate or build a CommonMark + GFM compliant parser that produces an AST (per D-MD-1). Must handle: headings, paragraphs, bold/italic/strikethrough, links, images, code spans, fenced code blocks with language info, blockquotes, ordered/unordered lists, task lists, tables, thematic breaks, autolinks. Must handle malformed/incomplete markdown gracefully (partial parse, not crash). Must be fast: parse a 10,000-line document in <100ms. Evaluate: swift-markdown, cmark-gfm Swift wrapper, or custom. AST must support round-trip (AST → markdown preserves original formatting). AST must include source positions (line:column ranges) for doctor indicators and cursor mapping.
acceptanceCriteria: Parser produces correct AST for all CommonMark + GFM spec examples
Malformed markdown produces a partial AST without crashing
AST round-trips to identical markdown (AST → markdown → AST produces same tree)
AST nodes include source position ranges (line:column start/end)
